discovery.run_summary: Risk-on tape with QQQ +1.66%, SMH +2.48%, VIX -6%, and META +4.7% leading mega-caps. Semiconductors and financials strong; energy and staples weak on oil -2% and PepsiCo miss. Mid-cap movers are dominated by semi-cap equipment sympathy strength and one large biotech breakout (BBIO) without a visible 8-K catalyst.

notable_index_moves: SMH +2.48% and XLK +2.18% leading, driving semi-cap equipment sympathy in ONTO, AEIS, VECO, LSCC
XLE -1.4% on oil -2% as Iran de-escalation hopes emerge
VIX -6% to 15.84 signals broad risk appetite return
KRE +1.79% shows small-cap/regional bank strength alongside IWM

discoveries.name: BridgeBio Pharma, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Large-cap biotech gapping on 4x volume with no visible SEC filing or news catalyst
discoveries.thesis: A sharp move on 4x average volume in a $17B biotech almost never happens without a specific catalyst (data readout, label expansion, partnership, or M&A chatter around Attruby/acoramidis). But with no 8-K and no news in our feed, we cannot commit to whether this is a legitimate re-rating or a squeeze/rumor that will fade.
discoveries.what_confirms: Company press release on ATTR-CM label/sales, partnership, or acquisition rumor; sell-side note raising peak sales for acoramidis
discoveries.what_kills: Move driven purely by sector rotation or a fund flow unwind with no company-specific news — would suggest fade risk
discoveries.what_to_learn: A 4x-volume double-digit move in a large-cap biotech without a same-day 8-K is a signal to check press releases and medical conference calendars manually before classifying — feed gaps are common in biotech
discoveries.catalyst: Unknown — no 8-K, no news items in feed, no scheduled earnings
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: catalyst_signals is empty {} despite a large move on very high volume — data gap
research_pointers: Check BBIO IR page for any acoramidis (Attruby) commercial update or label expansion
Search for ATTR-CM competitor readouts (PFE tafamidis, Alnylam) that might drive relative re-rating
Check option flow and short interest for squeeze dynamics
Look for any July medical conference presentations

discoveries.name: Onto Innovation Inc.
discoveries.setup: Semi-cap equipment name rebounding into SMH strength after prior week weakness
discoveries.thesis: ONTO is down ~10% over 5 days heading into today; SMH +2.48% and broader semi-equipment strength (AEIS, VECO, LSCC all +5%+) explain a sympathy bounce. Volume only 1.16x average — this is a beta rebound, not a company-specific event. Magnitude tracks the sector.
discoveries.what_confirms: Peer semi-cap names (AMAT, LRCX, KLAC) print similar-magnitude moves; no ONTO-specific news emerges
discoveries.what_kills: A specific ONTO catalyst (contract win, guide) surfaces — would make this UNDERDONE rather than RATIONAL
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals plus tight clustering of semi-equipment movers (AEIS +5.3%, VECO +5.5%, LSCC +5.3%) points to sector beta
research_pointers: Compare ONTO move to AMAT/LRCX/KLAC intraday for beta consistency
Check if any HBM/advanced packaging demand commentary emerged from a peer conference call

discoveries.name: AeroVironment, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Defense name selling off against flat ITA while broader tape is risk-on
discoveries.thesis: AVAV is down 5.9% today and 13.9% over 5 days while ITA is flat and SPY +0.85%. Iran de-escalation headlines pressure defense demand narrative, and Anduril CEO comments about IPO 'hype cycle' may be pulling drone/autonomy multiples lower. But without a company-specific 8-K, the weight of Iran ceasefire vs. Anduril commentary vs. some unseen catalyst is not clearly one-sided enough for a directional call.
discoveries.what_confirms: A specific 8-K emerges (contract loss, guidance cut) — would confirm bearish momentum continues
discoveries.what_kills: Move is purely Iran-ceasefire sympathy — mean-reverts once headlines fade; peer KTOS/RCAT show similar magnitude
discoveries.catalyst: Possible Iran de-escalation / defense multiple compression, but unconfirmed
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals; contextually oil -2% on Iran mediation suggests defense demand narrative softening
research_pointers: Check KTOS, RCAT, and other drone/loitering munition peers for magnitude comparison
Look for AVAV-specific news re: Switchblade contract updates or Ukraine funding
Review recent AVAV earnings guidance for any Q1 risk

discoveries.name: Intellia Therapeutics, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Gene-editing peer selloff with CRSP also down while XBI is up
discoveries.thesis: NTLA -8.3% and CRSP -4.8% together while XBI +0.8% indicates a gene-editing subgroup catalyst — possibly a safety/data event at one of the CRISPR programs or a competitive readout. Move magnitude is consistent with a mid-severity clinical concern spread across the two most correlated names.
discoveries.what_confirms: News of an adverse event, discontinuation, or competitor success in CRISPR/base-editing space
discoveries.what_kills: Isolated NTLA-specific pipeline setback — would make CRSP move sympathy-only and NTLA move justified
discoveries.what_to_learn: When two most-correlated peers move down together against a positive sector ETF, look for subgroup-specific news (modality, mechanism) before assuming sympathy
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Coordinated NTLA + CRSP weakness against XBI strength points to modality-specific news
research_pointers: Search for any NTLA-2001 (ATTR) or NTLA-2002 (HAE) trial updates
Check for competitor gene-editing readouts (VERV, BEAM)
Review FDA action calendar for CRISPR-related decisions

discoveries.name: JetBlue Airways Corporation
discoveries.setup: Low-cost airline rallying on oil -2% with ALK also strong
discoveries.thesis: JBLU +6% and ALK +5.5% into oil -2% is a classic jet-fuel-cost tailwind trade. Volume actually below average (0.77x), consistent with algorithmic sector-rotation rather than a specific JBLU catalyst. Magnitude is reasonable given JBLU's high fuel-cost sensitivity and low share price gearing.
discoveries.what_confirms: Oil continues lower; other airlines (LUV, UAL, DAL) show correlated moves
discoveries.what_kills: Iran headlines reverse and oil spikes back up; JBLU-specific bad news (route cuts, downgrade)
discoveries.catalyst: Oil -2% on Iran de-escalation, benefits fuel-heavy carriers
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: XLE -1.4% and oil -2% news alongside coordinated airline strength (JBLU, ALK)
research_pointers: Check UAL/DAL/LUV for magnitude comparison — JBLU/ALK gearing should be highest
Monitor whether JBLU 5-day is flat despite today's move (suggests recent weakness being unwound)

discoveries.name: Advanced Energy Industries, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Semi-cap equipment name bouncing after sharp 5-day drawdown against SMH strength
discoveries.thesis: AEIS -13.2% over 5 days into today's +5.3% is a partial recovery on light volume (0.69x). If the prior week's selloff was tied to a peer-driven derating without a company-specific catalyst, the bounce has room to run as SMH breaks out. The subdued volume suggests forced sellers are gone but buyers haven't fully returned.
discoveries.what_confirms: AEIS closes at day highs; peer semi-equipment names extend gains tomorrow; no negative 8-K emerges
discoveries.what_kills: A delayed 8-K reveals a company-specific reason for the 5-day weakness (guide cut, customer loss); SMH rolls over
discoveries.what_to_learn: Names down sharply over 5 days on no visible 8-K, bouncing on below-average volume with sector, often continue higher as underweights get covered
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals plus 5-day -13% vs. today's +5% on light volume suggests oversold bounce within sector recovery
research_pointers: Verify no pending 8-K risk (earnings warnings, mid-quarter update)
Check AEIS exposure to data-center power (industrial/semi split) — likely direct AI beneficiary
Compare to MKSI, IPGP for further semi-equipment names showing similar setup

ai_announcements.headline: Meta enters the crowded AI coding battle with Muse Spark 1.1
ai_announcements.summary: Meta launched an agentic AI coding tool targeting enterprise workloads, bug fixes, and code migrations, entering a space with GitHub Copilot, Cursor, Anthropic, and startups like Lovable.

ai_announcements.headline: Meta's new AI chips begin production in September
ai_announcements.summary: Meta advancing custom AI silicon with modular design approach, reducing reliance on Nvidia over time.

ai_announcements.headline: Anthropic's new Claude Reflect dashboard drives deeper AI usage habits
ai_announcements.summary: Product feature aimed at stickiness/engagement. Anthropic-related call flagged for bias grading. Not a direct threat to any named public company in today's tape.

ai_analysis.no_signals_note: None of today's largest single-stock moves have a clean, identifiable link to today's AI announcements. BBIO (+15%), NTLA (-8%), CRSP (-5%), VCYT, RGEN are biotech/diagnostics moves unrelated to AI news. AVAV (-6%) is defense/drones, unrelated. JBLU/ALK are airlines. The semi-equipment names (ONTO +8.8%, VECO +5.5%, AEIS +5.3%, LSCC +5.3%) are moving together and could reflect broader AI capex sentiment (Meta chip production news, general AI infra bid) but there is no company-specific catalyst tying them to a named announcement, and volume multiples are actually below average for most — suggests sector rotation/rebound after prior week weakness (VECO -18.5%, AEIS -13.2%, ONTO -9.8% over 5 days) rather than a fresh AI news catalyst. EXTR/CALX (networking) small-caps up modestly on no clear AI news link. No high-confidence 'AI news caused irrational move' setups today. Quiet AI-news-to-equity-move night.
